title: Arista Networks (ANET) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Arista Networks, Inc. (ANET) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1. Economic Prospect Score: 85/100 — Strong Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: An 18% growth rate is highly aggressive, but entirely justified by the structural tailwinds of AI networking. Arista is capturing outsized share in back-end AI networks where massive bandwidth is a hard requirement. Their operational efficiency ensures that top-line revenue growth drops directly to the bottom line, driving substantial cash flow expansion over the next five years.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: <div class="assumption-grid" data-astro-cid-ekmtdofa> <div class="assumption-card" data-astro-cid-ekmtdofa> <div class="card-title" data-astro-cid-ekmtdofa>FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: 3.5% slightly outpaces global GDP growth. The demand for bandwidth and low-latency networking is not a cyclical fad; it is a permanent, foundational requirement for the modern digital economy. Arista's sticky software ecosystem (EOS) ensures they will continue to extract value in perpetuity.

thesisTitle: The AI Thesis: Why I'm Bullish on the Build-Out
thesisParagraphs: Arista Networks (ANET) is currently perfectly positioned at the epicenter of the AI infrastructure boom. While companies like NVIDIA are supplying the compute engines, Arista is supplying the crucial connective tissue. The shift from general-purpose cloud computing to dense AI clusters demands a quantum leap in networking performance, and Arista's Ethernet solutions are rapidly taking share against proprietary alternatives like InfiniBand.

I view Arista not just as a hardware vendor, but as an operating system company (EOS) that happens to sell switches. This software-centric approach yields structurally higher gross margins than legacy competitors like Cisco. My valuation model assumes that as hyperscalers continue their massive, multi-year CapEx cycles into AI data centers, Arista's revenue growth will translate directly into explosive free cash flow generation.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini pick an 18% growth rate for Arista Networks?
faq.answer: Gemini projects an aggressive 18% Free Cash Flow growth rate because Arista is positioned as a primary beneficiary of the AI data center build-out. Their ethernet solutions are actively taking share in AI clusters, and their operational leverage allows revenue growth to translate directly into massive cash generation.

faq.question: What discount rate was used for ANET's DCF?
faq.answer: A 10.0% discount rate (WACC) was selected. This is calculated using CAPM with a 4.18% 10-Year US Treasury yield and Arista's high beta of 1.46. The elevated rate accounts for the concentration risk associated with their major cloud titan customers.

faq.question: Is it safe to rely on AI for stock valuation?
faq.answer: No. This analysis is a demonstration of AI reasoning based on a specific set of inputs and rigid formulas. It is not financial advice. AI models cannot predict macroeconomic shifts, customer losses, or sudden technological obsolescence.

prospectVerdictDetail: Arista Networks continues to deliver exceptional financial performance, driven by its dominance in high-speed datacenter switching. The firm's software-driven approach and close partnerships with major cloud providers create a deeply entrenched ecosystem. With robust revenue growth and an impressive 31.4% ROE, Arista is highly leveraged to the ongoing secular shift toward complex, software-defined network architectures. Although the stock commands a premium valuation, the underlying business quality remains pristine.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: Arista's competitive momentum is exceptional, driven by structural advantages in datacenter architecture and aggressive market share gains.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: Arista significantly outpaces legacy networking competitors. Its focus on high-performance cloud computing environments allows the firm to capture outsized growth as capital expenditures pivot aggressively toward these segments.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: The company continues to take meaningful share in the 100G+ datacenter switching market. Its close alignment with 'cloud titan' customers ensures Arista remains at the forefront of the architectural transitions driving the industry.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: Arista wields substantial pricing power through its integrated hardware and software solutions. The total cost of ownership (TCO) advantages of its Extensible Operating System (EOS) allow the company to command premium margins.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: The firm consistently introduces cutting-edge networking silicon and software features, maintaining a rapid product velocity that ensures it meets the escalating bandwidth demands of massive hyperscale architectures.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: Arista's moat is built on high switching costs associated with its proprietary EOS software and the sheer complexity of ripping and replacing core datacenter infrastructure.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Once a cloud provider standardizes on Arista's EOS for its critical network fabric, the cost, risk, and operational friction of migrating to an alternative vendor are prohibitive. This lock-in ensures highly recurring revenue streams.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: While not a consumer network, Arista benefits from an ecosystem effect. The widespread adoption of EOS by major cloud providers creates a de facto standard, incentivizing third-party developers and integration partners to prioritize the platform.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: Arista holds a strong portfolio of networking patents, crucial for defending its high-margin business against legacy incumbents. However, the sector is highly competitive and subject to ongoing intellectual property litigation.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: By leveraging merchant silicon and a software-centric model, Arista operates with significantly less capital intensity than traditional, vertically integrated networking hardware manufacturers, supporting its high ROE.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Market sentiment for Arista remains strong, driven by broad datacenter tailwinds, although valuation concerns occasionally temper the narrative.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Analysts consistently revise estimates upward as Arista repeatedly beats expectations, supported by the relentless demand from cloud titans upgrading their network fabrics.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The narrative focuses on Arista's indispensable role in powering the next generation of cloud infrastructure. Recent news highlights the firm maintaining key technical levels amidst a brightening outlook, despite occasional sector-wide volatility.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Under the leadership of Jayshree Ullal, management has executed flawlessly since the IPO. The company's focused capital allocation toward R&D ensures sustained technological leadership in a rapidly evolving market.

prospectKeyRisks: Customer concentration is a significant risk. A substantial portion of Arista's revenue is derived from a small number of massive 'cloud titan' customers; a shift in spending by even one could materially impact growth.
The broader networking sector is subject to cyclical capital expenditure trends among large enterprises and hyperscalers, which can lead to periods of lumpy revenue growth.
Intense competition from established legacy vendors and the potential emergence of disruptive 'white box' or open-source networking solutions.
prospectKeyCatalysts: The AI infrastructure buildout is projected to sustain $150B+ in annual hyperscaler capex through 2028, with networking representing 10-15% of total spend — a massive and growing addressable market for Arista

Campus networking expansion into Cisco's $15B enterprise switching market is still early innings, and Arista's competitive wins at large enterprises are accelerating as organizations modernize legacy Cisco Cat6K/9K estates
The transition from 400G to 800G and eventually 1.6T Ethernet creates a multi-year upgrade cycle that benefits Arista's premium positioning in high-performance switching
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored ANET at 88/100 and Opus at 85/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
